title: Quality Department
learning_objectives: Understand the role of the Quality Department.
Learn different stages of quality inspection.
Understand quality assurance and quality control concepts.
Recognize the importance of continuous improvement.
Understand the role of SAP QM in quality management.

introduction.title: What is the Quality Department?
introduction.content: The Quality Department ensures that products meet customer expectations, company specifications and regulatory requirements. Through inspections, testing and continuous monitoring, the department prevents defects, improves manufacturing processes and supports customer satisfaction.

business_purpose.title: Why is the Quality Department Important?
business_purpose.content: Quality directly influences customer trust and business reputation. Effective quality management reduces defects, minimizes rework, lowers production costs and ensures that only products meeting required standards reach customers.

quality_workflow: Record inspection results in SAP QM.
Analyze quality data for continuous improvement.

types_of_quality_inspection.description: Checks raw materials received from suppliers before they enter production.
Verifies finished products before shipment to customers.
Analyzes customer feedback and identifies corrective actions.

key_responsibilities.description: Inspect raw materials, work-in-progress and finished products.
Perform mechanical, dimensional and visual quality checks.
Maintain inspection reports and quality records.
Identify, isolate and manage defective products.
Recommend process improvements to reduce defects.
Investigate customer complaints and implement corrective actions.

quality_concepts.description: Focuses on preventing defects through systematic processes.
Focuses on detecting defects through inspections and testing.
Corrective and Preventive Actions eliminate root causes of quality issues.
Identifies the actual reason behind recurring quality problems.

coordination_with_departments.purpose: Ensures products meet specifications during manufacturing.
Approves incoming and finished material storage.
Reports equipment issues affecting product quality.
Records inspection lots, results and usage decisions.

business_example.title: Quality Operations at JSL
business_example.content: A batch of stainless steel coils is received from production. Quality inspectors perform dimensional measurements, surface finish inspection and mechanical property testing. The inspection results are entered into SAP QM. Products meeting all specifications receive an acceptance decision and are released for dispatch, while non-conforming coils are isolated for corrective action.

common_errors: Poor communication between Production and Quality.
important_notes: Quality should be built into the process, not only inspected at the end.
Every employee contributes to product quality.
Customer satisfaction depends on consistent product quality.
Continuous improvement is an ongoing process.
